VitalDB clinical_data.csv shows caseend=11542 for case 0001; 11542 seconds = 3 hours 12 minutes 22 seconds (192 minutes 22 seconds)
VitalDB clinical_data.csv shows caseend=15741 for case 0002; 15741 seconds = 4 hours 22 minutes 21 seconds (262 minutes 21 seconds). Metadata claims 20s, actual is 21s (1 second discrepancy).

pi_items.metadata_value: VitalDB open dataset (Seoul National University Hospital)
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB - open dataset from Seoul National University Hospital containing 6,388 surgical cases with high-fidelity vital signs monitoring data collected Aug 2016 - Jun 2017
pi_items.source: Scientific Data publication (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/) and PhysioNet repository (https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real surgical case data from the VitalDB open dataset maintained by Seoul National University Hospital. Case 0001 is a 3-hour 12-minute 22-second noncardiac surgical recording with arterial pressure (ART), multiple ECG leads, pulse oximetry (PLETH), capnography (CO2), and sevoflurane anesthetic agent monitoring. Case 0002 is a 4-hour 22-minute 21-second noncardiac surgical recording featuring ECG, pulse oximetry, heart rate, ST segment monitoring, and ventilator parameters. The case files are publicly available on PhysioNet as part of the VitalDB v1.0.0 dataset.
